harnessed.explanation_en: to control and make use of something
harnessed.example_sentence: We need to harness the power of the wind to create electricity.
harnessed.original_sentence: Ideas are fruits of your thinking, but they've got to be harnessed and put to work to have value.

put to work.explanation_en: to make something useful or start using it
put to work.example_sentence: He finally put his new skills to work in his office.
put to work.original_sentence: Ideas are fruits of your thinking, but they've got to be harnessed and put to work to have value.

populate.explanation_en: to live in or fill a place
populate.example_sentence: Many different species populate this small island.
populate.original_sentence: Each year, an oak tree produces enough acorns to populate a good-sized forest.

bear fruit.example_sentence: After months of hard work, our plans finally began to bear fruit.

perishable.explanation_en: likely to decay or go bad quickly
perishable.example_sentence: Milk and fruit are perishable goods.

on guard.explanation_en: careful and prepared for danger or difficulty
on guard.example_sentence: You must be on guard against making simple mistakes.
on guard.original_sentence: If we're not on guard, the squirrels, negative-thinking people, will destroy most of them.

nailed to paper.explanation_en: to write something down firmly so it is not forgotten
nailed to paper.example_sentence: Don't just think about your goals; get them nailed to paper.
nailed to paper.original_sentence: Every day lots of good ideas are born only to die quickly because they aren't nailed to paper.

nurturing.explanation_en: helping something to develop and grow
nurturing.example_sentence: She is good at nurturing talent in her students.
nurturing.original_sentence: Memory is a weak slave when it comes to preserving and nurturing brand new ideas.

the instant.explanation_en: the exact moment when something happens
the instant.example_sentence: I knew the answer the instant he asked the question.
the instant.original_sentence: A friend who travels a lot keeps a clipboard beside him so that he can write down an idea the instant it occurs to him.

fertile.explanation_en: producing many new and creative ideas
fertile.example_sentence: She has a fertile imagination and writes wonderful stories.
fertile.original_sentence: People with fertile creative minds know a good idea may sprout any time, any place.

sprout.explanation_en: to start to grow or appear suddenly
sprout.example_sentence: New cafes are sprouting up all over the city.
sprout.original_sentence: People with fertile creative minds know a good idea may sprout any time, any place.

fence in.explanation_en: to keep something within a limited area or under control
fence in.example_sentence: We need to fence in the garden to keep the dog safe.

elaborate.explanation_en: containing many carefully arranged parts or details
elaborate.example_sentence: They made elaborate preparations for the wedding.
elaborate.original_sentence: The file can be an elaborate cabinet, or it can be a desk drawer.

go over.explanation_en: to examine or check something carefully
go over.example_sentence: Please go over your essay for spelling mistakes.
go over.original_sentence: As you go over your ideas, some may, for very good reasons, have no value at all.

get rid of.explanation_en: to throw away or remove something you do not want
get rid of.example_sentence: It is time to get rid of these old clothes.

akin to.example_sentence: His feeling of loss was akin to grief.
akin to.original_sentence: Read anything you can find that is in any way akin to your idea.

the time is ripe.explanation_en: the perfect time to do something
the time is ripe.example_sentence: The time is ripe to start your own business.
the time is ripe.original_sentence: Then, when the time is ripe, put it to work for yourself, your job, your future.
